title: Strangeness Physics with CLAS at JLab
abstract: A brief overview of strangeness physics with the CLAS detector at JLab is given, mainly covering the domain of nucleon resonances. Several excited states predicted by the symmetric constituent quark model may have significant couplings to the K-Lambda or K-Sigma channels. I will discuss data that are relevant in the search for such states in the strangeness channel, and give an outlook on the future prospects of the N* program at JLab with electromagnetic probes.
comment: 9 pages, 7 figures, Conference on Strangeness in Nuclei, SENDAI 2008
